en: Welcome back, everybody, and ladies and gentlemen, folks, my next guest is an Emmy winner and
ko: 다시 오신 걸 환영합니다.

en: Tony nominee who has performed in four different languages in over 45 countries.
ko: 다음 게스트는 에미상 수상자이자 토니상 후보로, 4개 언어로 45개국 이상에서 공연한 분입니다.

en: She now stars in a solo stage production of Great Expectations here in New York.
ko: 현재 뉴욕에서 위대한 유산 1인극을 하고 있습니다.

en: Please welcome back to The Late Show, Eddie Izzard.
ko: 에디 이자드를 다시 환영합니다.

en: Nice to see you. Very good to be here.
ko: 만나서 반가워요. 여기 오니 좋네요.

en: Now, the last time we were together was 2017.
ko: 마지막으로 만난 게 2017년이었죠.

en: And at the time, if you remember, you said within four to five years, and it has been five years,
ko: 그때 4~5년 안에 국회의원에 출마하겠다고 했는데,

en: you would be running for Parliament.
ko: 5년이 됐네요.

en: Yeah. And you are one of your word, because on Sunday you had your primary election.
ko: 네. 약속을 지키셨네요. 일요일에 예비선거가 있었죠.

en: Here's a pamphlet. This proves you're running for office. Yes.
ko: 여기 전단지가 있어요. 출마 증거죠. 하지만 덧붙여야 해요.

en: But we should add to this. Primary. Primary was Sunday. How did it go?
ko: 하지만 이걸 더해야 해. 프라이머리. 프라이머리는 일요일이었어. 어땠어?

en: I didn't get it. I came second. But it's called The Great People in the City of Sheffield.
ko: 예비선거가 일요일이었는데 어떻게 됐어요? 안 됐어요. 2등이었어요. 셰필드 시의 멋진 사람들이에요.

en: Sheffield is a steel city. Yes. And people who know Sheffield internationally, you may not know Sheffield so well.
ko: 셰필드는 철강 도시예요. 국제적으로는 잘 모르시겠지만 마음이 따뜻한 도시예요.

en: But it's got a big heart. It's got great people, great character.
ko: 하지만 그곳은 큰 마음을 가지고 있어. 좋은 사람들, 훌륭한 인격이 있어.

en: It was a steel city. Then the steel industry kind of went away from Britain.
ko: 훌륭한 사람들, 훌륭한 성격의 도시예요. 철강 도시였다가 철강 산업이 떠났죠.

en: And they've reinvented themselves.
ko: 첨단 기술,

en: High energy, high engineering jobs and two great universities.
ko: 고급 엔지니어링 직업과 훌륭한 대학교 두 곳으로 재탄생했어요.

en: A lot of great talent there. So, yeah, I still... It's where I went to.
ko: 인재가 많아요. 네, 아직도...

en: When I went to my degree, when I was doing accounting and financial management,
ko: 제가 학위를 하러 간 곳이에요. 회계 재무관리를 했는데 무서웠죠.

en: I was scared. And someone was there as well who remembers my degree. You have an accounting degree?
ko: 나는 무서웠어. 그리고 내 학위를 기억하는 누군가도 있었어. 너 회계학 학위가 있어?

en: Well, I dropped... Something to fall back on? I dropped out after one year, just like Steve Jobs and Bill Gates did.
ko: 회계학 학위가 있어요? 1년 만에 중퇴했어요. 스티브 잡스와 빌 게이츠처럼요.

en: Very similar. That's the group I've shoved myself in. Well, I knew I wanted to perform.
ko: 비슷하죠. 그 그룹에 끼어들었어요.

en: When did you know you wanted to perform? Well, when I ended up being the youngest of 11 children.
ko: 공연하고 싶다는 걸 알았어요. 언제 공연하고 싶다는 걸 알았어요?

en: That's when I found out. Oh, right. OK. Exactly. There was always an audience there.
ko: 11형제 중 막내가 됐을 때. 그때 알았죠. 항상 관객이 있었으니까요.

en: Seven years old. Mum had died when I was six. Seven years old.
ko: 7살이었어요. 엄마가 6살 때 돌아가셨어요.

en: I lost the love and affection of Mum.
ko: 엄마의 사랑을 잃었는데 관객이 사랑을 줬어요.

en: And suddenly there was an audience giving out love and affection. I said, I think I need that.
ko: 그리고 갑자기 사랑과 애정이 넘치는 관객이 있었어. 나는, 그게 필요하다고 생각했어.

en: And it just locked in. And then I had to pretend, yes, I'll go to university.
ko: 그게 필요하다고 생각했어요. 그냥 딱 맞았어요. 대학에 가는 척해야 했죠.

en: But I was never going to university. Did you get it? Did you get that? Because a lot of people...
ko: 대학에 갈 생각이 없었어요. 공감하세요?

en: I lost my father and a couple of my brothers when I was quite young.
ko: 저도 어린 시절에 아버지와 형들을 잃었거든요.

en: And that actually led me to, like, try to keep my mum happy.
ko: 그래서 엄마를 행복하게 하려고 했어요.

en: That was sort of the beginning of my performance for me, I think, when I was around 10.
ko: 제 공연의 시작이었던 것 같아요, 열 살쯤.

en: But did you then... Did that supplant that love
ko: 그래서...

en: or did that feed that missing piece of you?
ko: 그 빈자리를 채워줬나요, 아니면 유령을 쫓는 거였나요?

en: Sincerely, I mean. Or was it like a ghost you were chasing?
ko: 유령을 쫓는 거였다고 생각해요. 진심으로요.

en: I think it was a ghost I was chasing. I've tried to do amazing things.
ko: 놀라운 것들을 하려고 했어요.

en: You know, I've been running lots of multiple marathons and stuff and performing, as you said, in multiple languages.
ko: 마라톤도 많이 뛰고, 여러 언어로 공연도 하고요.

en: And in my documentary that was done on me,
ko: 다큐멘터리에서,

en: it was about trying to do enough so that maybe Mum could see that stuff was happening that was different
ko: 엄마가 저 너머에서 볼 수 있도록 뭔가

en: and she could see from beyond, you know, wherever.
ko: 다른 일이 일어나는 걸 보여주려는 거였어요.

en: But I unfortunately don't think, you know... I think Mum's gone.
ko: 하지만 안타깝게도... 엄마는 떠나셨다고 생각해요. 하지만 여기 안에 계시고, 저는 트랜스예요.

en: But she's in here and I'm trans. I'm not trans because of Mum, you know.
ko: 하지만 그녀는 여기 있고 나는 트랜스야. 엄마 때문에 트랜스가 된 건 아니야, 알지?

en: I was just trans before that. I knew when I was about four or five.
ko: 엄마 때문에 트랜스가 된 건 아니에요. 그 전부터 트랜스였어요. 4~5살 때 알았어요.

en: So, yeah, but I've just tried to live a positive life, being honest, being out for 37 years,
ko: 긍정적인 삶을 살려고 했어요. 정직하게, 37년간 커밍아웃하고, 연기하고 스탠드업 코미디를 하면서요.

en: acting and doing stand-up comedy, which are quite different things, you know? Very much so.
ko: 연기와 스탠드업 코미디는 꽤 다른 거야, 알지? 정말 많이 달라.

en: I never did stand-up. I admire people who've done it because it seems like a bit of a lonely thing at times.
ko: 정말 그렇죠. 저는 스탠드업을 안 해봤어요. 존경합니다. 외로운 일 같거든요. 그럴 수 있죠.

en: It can... Yeah, I think it can be. I'm quite happy out there cos I feel I'm with the audience.
ko: 그럴 수 있어... 응, 그럴 수 있을 것 같아. 나는 관객과 함께 있는 것 같아서 꽤 행복해.

en: Cos I'm talking... In my stand-up, I'm talking complete nonsense about pigs in space and playing banjos and stuff.
ko: 무대에 나가면 관객과 함께라서 편해요. 우주의 돼지가 밴조 치는 얘기같은 말도 안 되는 얘기를 하거든요.

en: And the audience is there and it's like everyone's coming to my room and we're all just playing around.
ko: 관객이 있으면 모두 제 방에 놀러 온 것 같아요. 연극은 달라요.

en: In drama, it's a different thing. Like, The Dickens is a drama. It's not a comedy.
ko: 드라마에서는 다른 거야. 예를 들어, The Dickens는 드라마야. 코미디가 아니야.

en: Let's get to that right here cos what we've got right here is that you're also, besides running for office...
ko: 디킨즈는 드라마예요. 코미디가 아닌데, 지금 뉴욕 그리니치하우스 극장에서 하고 있죠.

en: Yes, I do. I do want to ask you about that in just a moment, about that, but you are also at the Greenwich House Theatre
ko: 디킨즈 클래식 위대한 유산의 1인극이요.

en: here downtown in New York. You are doing a one-person adaptation
ko: 여기 뉴욕 다운타운에서. 너는 1인 극복을 하고 있어.

en: of the Dickens classic Great Expectations.
ko: 네. 디킨즈 팬들이네요. 오늘 밤 디킨즈 팬이 많네요.

en: Yeah. OK, so, I... APPLAUSE
ko: 패트릭 스튜어트도 크리스마스 캐롤을 했었죠.

en: Dickens fans. A lot of Dickens fans here tonight.
ko: 好幾個人演過《聖誕頌歌》。獨角戲的概念以前就有了。

en: Well, people have already been...
ko: 여러 사람이 크리스마스 캐롤을 했어요.

en: Patrick Stewart has been doing Christmas Carol. A number of people have been doing Christmas Carol.
ko: 1인극은 전에도 있었죠.

en: Simon Callow in Britain. So, the idea of doing solo shows has happened before.
ko: 리처드 프라이어의 스탠드업 기법을 쓰고 있어요. 살짝 다른 목소리, 자세, 태도로 캐릭터를 바꾸는 거죠.

en: It's just the interesting thing on this is that I'm using Richard Pryor's stand-up technique,
ko: 리처드 프라이어 라이브를 보면, 두 사람이 사냥 가는 장면에서 총 줘,

en: which has infused my stand-up, which is multi...
ko: 총이 없는데?

en: Well, Richard used to do this thing. If you watch Richard Pryor live, you can see him...
ko: 음, 리차드가 이런 걸 하곤 했어. 리차드 프라이어 라이브를 보면 그를 볼 수 있어...

en: I think it's talking about two African-American guys going hunting and one guy going, OK, give me the gun.
ko: 두 캐릭터가 살짝 다른 목소리와 자세로 대화하는 거예요.

en: He says, I haven't got the gun. You haven't got the gun? Where's the gun?
ko: 그래서 그걸 좋아하겠다고 생각했고, 제 스탠드업에 적용했어요.

en: And it's this talking of two characters, slightly different voices, different postures,
ko: 디킨즈에서도 드라마틱하게 적용할 수 있겠다 싶었어요.

en: different attitudes, talking to each other with this sort of quarter turn.
ko: 에스텔라, 미스 해비샴, 핍이 대화하죠.

en: And so I thought, I like that. I come from sketch comedy, I'll do that in my stand-up.
ko: 태도,

en: So, I've done it all over my stand-up. And then I realised with the Dickens, I could do it in the Dickens dramatically.
ko: 목소리를 바꾸면서 모든 캐릭터가 대화하게 해요.

en: So, I have Estella, Miss Havisham and Pip all talking to each other with this thing.
ko: 5~6명까지도요. 헷갈린 적 있어요? 네, 항상. 무대에서요.

en: I change the attitude, the voice, and I just get all the characters talking to each other that way.
ko: 무대에서요. 자, 티켓 팔아봅시다, 기대감을 높여야죠.

en: Sometimes up to five or six characters. Do you ever get confused? Yes, all the time.
ko: 위대한 기대감을요. 얼마나 위대한 기대인가요? 줄거리를 말해줄까요?

en: LAUGHTER On stage, I mean. On stage, I mean. Yes, yes.
ko: 웃음 무대에서, 말이야. 무대에서, 말이야. 맞아, 맞아.

en: Oh, yeah, let's come on, let's sell some tickets, let's build expectations for this.
ko: 모르시는 분을 위해, 대장장이 견습생이 돈을 받는데 누구한테 받는지 모르는 이야기예요.

en: Build great expectations for this. OK. Yes. How great are these expectations?
ko: 큰집에 사는 까다로운 노부인과 아름다운 양녀에게서 온 줄 알았는데 아니에요.

en: The expectations... Well, you want me to tell the story of how it goes?
ko: 他學到了教訓，試圖在故事結束時成為一個正派的人。

en: If you don't know the story, it's kind of a...
ko: 돈을 펑펑 쓰면서 돈이 행복을 줄 거라 생각하지만 결국은 그렇지

en: Well, it's a rags-to-riches story of a little kid who was growing up as an apprentice to a blacksmith,
ko: 않아요.

en: and he gets given all this money, he doesn't know who it's from.
ko: 我們這裡也有政治。我不想談我國家的政治，

en: He thinks it's from a rich lady who lives in a... Cranky old lady who lives up in a house,
ko: 교훈을 배우고 이야기 끝에 괜찮은 사람이 되려고 합니다.

en: who has a beautiful adopted daughter, but it isn't.
ko: 如你所說，你不孤單，因為他們在劇場和你在一起。

en: And he becomes this guy who's throwing money left, right and centre.
ko: 더 발전된 후기 작품이고,

en: He thinks money's going to make him happy. It doesn't, in the end.
ko: 위대한 서사시예요.

en: And he learns the lessons, and he tries to become a decent human being by the end of the story.
ko: 정치 이야기를 잠깐만 더 할게요. 당신 나라에도 정치가 있죠.

en: It's one of his more advanced stories, his later stories, and it's a great epic.
ko: 여기도 정치가 있죠. 우리나라 정치는 말하고 싶지 않지만, 성공적인 경력이 있잖아요.

en: Well, I want to ask you back about this politics for just one second here,
ko: 코미디로 사람들을 더 좋게 만들고 있잖아요. 극장에서 관객과 함께니까요.

en: because you have politics in your country too. We do have politics here.
ko: 너희 나라에도 정치가 있으니까. 여기에도 정치가 있어.

en: And I don't want to talk about politics in my country,
ko: 왜 정치를 하려고 해요? 이 사람들과 시간을 보내봤어요?

en: other than the fact, other than this, you have a successful career.
ko: 옳은 일을 하려는 사람을 존경하지만 거의 못 만나거든요.

en: You make things better for people by doing your comedy.
ko: 영국 정치가 미국 정치와 다른가요?

en: As you said, you're not alone, they're not alone, because they're with you in the theatre.
ko: 같아요.

en: Why do you want to do politics? Have you spent time with these people?
ko: 거짓말을 하기로 한 정치인들이 있죠.

en: Because I admire people who are trying to do the right thing, I just meet so few of them.
ko: 很瘋狂，但是很美好的事。你的膝蓋還好嗎？奇怪的是還好。

en: Is your politics different than our politics? No, it's the same thing.
ko: 트럼프가 있고, 우리에겐 보리스 존슨이 있었어요. 뻔뻔한 거짓말 정치의 트위들덤과 트위들디.

en: I mean, there are certain politicians, in inverted commas you've turned up,
ko: 성격과 자아가 있는 사람들을 끌어들여서 그냥 거짓말로 밀고 나가는 거예요.

en: who decided that they could do lying instead of telling the truth.
ko: 死後會更難，但也許有人可以推著我跑。

en: And you've got a Donald Trump on your side, and we had a Boris Johnson on our side.
ko: 특정 지지층에 호소하죠. 저는 급진적이지만 온건합니다.

en: The Tweedledum and Tweedledee of pants-on-fire politics.
ko: 급진적인 일을 하지만요.

en: Yes. You know? And it kind of attracts people who have personality,
ko: 급진적으로 온건하다고요?

en: who have ego, but just decide, I'm just going to lie my way through the whole of this.
ko: 마라톤 130번 뛰었지만 영국과 세계 자선단체를 위해서예요.

en: And it appeals to certain people, because they appeal to a certain base.
ko: 의도는 온건하지만 행동은 급진적이죠.

en: And I'm a radical, but a moderate. I do radical things. Radically moderate?
ko: 남아공에서 넬슨 만델라를 위해 27일간 27번 마라톤을 뛰었어요.

en: Yeah, well, I run 130 marathons, but I'm making money for charities in Britain and around the world.
ko: 대단하지만 아름다운 일이죠. 무릎은 괜찮아요? 이상하게 괜찮아요.

en: That's pretty moderate in intention, but it's a radical thing to do.
ko: 왜인지 모르겠지만 무릎이 계속 버텨요. 아직도 달리고 있어요?

en: I mean, in South Africa, I did 27 marathons in 27 days for Nelson Mandela.
ko: 네. 영원히 마라톤을 뛸 거예요.

en: That's out there, but, you know, it's a beautiful thing. And salutes Nelson Mandela.
ko: 영원히는 아니고.

en: How are your knees? My knees are fine, bizarrely.
ko: 죽고 나면 어렵겠지만 누가 밀어줄 수도 있잖아요.

en: I don't know why, but they... My knees keep going.
ko: 재를 뿌려서? 모르겠어요.

en: Are they still running? Yeah. Oh, yes. I'm going to keep doing marathons forever, until I shuffle off. Not forever.
ko: 에너지가 있고, 분석력이 있어요.

en: Well, I can do... After death, it's going to be harder, but maybe someone can push me around.
ko: 37년 전 트랜스로 커밍아웃해서 리더십을 보여주려 했어요. 다른 트랜스인들이 나올 수 있도록요.

en: They can push me around. Rub the ashes on them, and then they run around? Yeah, I don't know.
ko: 그래서 앞으로 나아갈 수 있었어요. 85년에 커밍아웃했을 때 트랜스에 대해 아무도 이야기하지 않았거든요.

en: So, this is... I believe I have energy, I have analysis.
ko: 저도 에디 이자드 같은 사람이라고 생각하고 싶어요.

en: You know, I came out as trans 37 years ago, trying to show leadership and say, I'm trying to make a space for myself.
ko: 좋은 의미로요. 다른 방식을 시도하면서 밀고 나가는 거요.

en: And if anyone else is thinking, I'm trans and they want to come out, maybe they say, I'm a bit like that person.
ko: 좋은 곳에 도달한 다음 다른 사람을 도울 수 있는지.

en: And so we could move forward, because we weren't... No-one was even discussing trans back in 85, when I came out.
ko: 다른 사람을 끌어올릴 수 있는지? 관계를 끊지 않고 만드는 거죠.

en: Well, I'd like to think of myself as a bit like Eddie Izzard.
ko: 배울 수 있는지?

en: Well, good, in a positive way. In a positive way, yes. Trying things different ways, pushing out.
ko: 배울 수 있을까요? 그게 제 정치예요.

en: Yeah, because there's a number of us out there trying to make... We're trying to get ourselves in a good place,
ko: 의회에 들어갈 때까지 계속 밀겠습니다.

en: then trying to reach out. Can we help anyone else? Can we help bring people up?
ko: 그럼 도움을 줄 수 있을까? 다른 사람들을 도와줄 수 있을까? 사람들을 끌어올릴 수 있을까?

en: You know, make connections rather than break them.
ko: 에디, 만나서 반가웠어요. 위대한 유산은 내일부터 뉴욕 그리니치하우스 극장에서 합니다.

en: Can we learn from you? Can you learn from us? That's my politics.
ko: 우리는 너에게서 배울 수 있을까? 너는 우리에게서 배울 수 있을까? 그게 내 정치야.

en: So, I'm just going to keep pushing until I get into Parliament,
ko: 에디 이자드였습니다.

en: and then I can keep trying to make things better for people.
ko: 알리샤 키스의

en: Eddie, lovely to see you. Yeah, see you.
ko: 공연으로 돌아옵니다.

en: Great Expectations begins tomorrow at the Greenwich House Theatre here in New York.
ko: 알리샤 키스의 공연으로 돌아옵니다.

en: Eddie Izzard, everybody. We'll be right back with a performance by Alicia Keys.
ko: 에디 이자드, 여러분. 알리샤 키스의 공연이 이어집니다.
